post.description: Imports a new storage. Specify a name that will override the name of the imported element, and the binary content.

parameters.description: Parameter to override the name of the imported element
parameters.name: name

parameters.description: WARNING Please let data factory server generate the unique identifier for you, you can set this value to null this is only for advanced and controlled use cases. Parameter to override the identifier of the imported element. Important the identifier should contain only alphanumeric characters. Id should match the following regex "^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]*$. Besides size of the identifier should higher than 2 characters and not exceeds 256 characters. The value of the id should be unique cross tenant"
parameters.name: identifier

parameters.description:
When importing a resource with an existing `resource_id` or `resource_uuid`, this flag controls whether the resource is updated.
  - If `true`, the import process will update the matching resource in the current workspace.
  - If `false` (default), the import will be rejected if a resource with the same `resource_id` or `resource_uuid` already exists.
**Note:** Update is only allowed for specific users with proper permissions. When `resource_uuid` is provided and exists in a different workspace, the resource will be moved and updated into the current workspace (if `update=true`).
This parameter does **not** allow clearing or destructive updates to existing items â€” see `forceUpdate` for IndexUnit-specific behavior.
parameters.name: update

parameters.description:
For index unit storage only, if true when updating an existing index unit the import endpoint will automatically
clear impacted items if data model modifications contains breaking changes
(ex : delete or update of existing attribute or class). 
If false, the import will fail if items are impacted at update. 
This feature activation is restricted only for semantic graph index technical user
parameters.name: forceUpdate

parameters.description:
Parameter to override the workspace identifier in which the resource will be imported. 
Default value used is the Global Data Factory Default instance at import create and at import create will use the current workspace of the resource. 
In case of import update the resource is in a workspace A and at import update this parameter is set to an existing workspace B, 
then the resource will move from the workspace A to the workspace B.
parameters.name: workspaceId

405.description: Forbid to force update index unit from crawler manager

409.description:
Storage cannot be updated because it has conflicts :
- When updating index unit datamodel with breaking data model changes regarding existing items in the index unit. Please clear these impacted items before calling update.
- When import update parameter is true and force update parameter is false ; if index unit storage update contains breaking changes return code is 409 conflict</br>
- When import update parameter is false and the storage already exist return a 409 status error"

post.description:
Move an existing resource from a workspace to another existing workspace. 
Proceed with caution. 
This operation may impact existing resources that rely on this storage, both in the current data workspace and in dependent workspaces. 
If a resource with the same identifier exists in the target workspace, it will be overwritten. 
All pipelines referencing this storage will be stopped after the move, and their configurations will be updated to point to the storage in the new target data project. 
During the move, both storage data and configuration history will be preserved.

post.summary: Index unit ingestion endpoint

post.description:
Initiates an asynchronous data consolidation process that executes the input data query and ingests events generated by the selected export outputs.
#### Specifications

- The HTTP request returns once the job is validated, registered in the database, it will be then be submitted asynchronously.
- The actual query execution and ingestion happen asynchronously.
- A `jobId` is provided in the response to monitor the execution status.
- The data consolidation query has a **30-minute timeout**.
- The data consolidation query execution is single-threaded.
- A maximum of **5 concurrent workers** can perform consolidations in data factory.
- If the worker pool is full and too many consolidation request are pending, the request is rejected with a **429 (Too Many Requests)** response.
- The query runs within the target index unit workspace, leveraging its available resource dependencies.
- If the target storage is deleted, all associated consolidation query will be canceled 

#### Optional Behavior: Job Cancellation and Consolidation Scope Management
- The request body may include an optional `consolidationId`.
- This identifier represents a logical consolidation scope (e.g., a specific business process).
- If another consolidation job is already running with the same `consolidationId`, it will be **automatically cancelled** before the new job is submitted.
- This mechanism ensures that at most **one active job per consolidationId** is processed at a given time.
- Each call to this endpoint generates a new system-level `jobId`, which uniquely identifies the submitted job and is returned in the response.
- The `jobId` must be used to retrieve the job's status or cancel the job

get.description:
Returns a list of pipeline notifications, given skip and top parameters. if skip = 5 and top = 1
this service will return in order notifications with skips : [5]

if skip = 5 and top = 3
this service will return in order notifications with skips : [5, 4, 3]

if skip > MAX or < 0
this service will return no notifications, but the count will be computed

if skip = 20 and top = 10
this service will return in order notifications with skips : [20, 18, ..., 11]

if skip is not set, and top = 10
this service will return the 10 last notifications in the queue, ex : [103, 102, 101, ... 94]

if skip = 5, and top = 10
this service will return the 6 notifications in the queue, ex : [5, 4, 3, ..., 0]

ImportUpdate.description:
When importing a resource with an existing `resource_id` or `resource_uuid`, this flag controls whether the resource is updated.
  - If `true`, the import process will update the matching resource in the current workspace.
  - If `false` (default), the import will be rejected if a resource with the same `resource_id` or `resource_uuid` already exists.
**Note:** Update is only allowed for specific users with proper permissions. When `resource_uuid` is provided and exists in a different workspace, the resource will be moved and updated into the current workspace (if `update=true`).
This parameter does **not** allow clearing or destructive updates to existing items â€” see `forceUpdate` for IndexUnit-specific behavior.
ImportUpdate.name: update

ImportForceUpdate.description:
For index unit storage only, if true when updating an existing index unit the import endpoint will automatically
clear impacted items if data model modifications contains breaking changes
(ex : delete or update of existing attribute or class). 
If false, the import will fail if items are impacted at update. 
This feature activation is restricted only for semantic graph index technical user
ImportForceUpdate.name: forceUpdate

then the resource will move from the workspace A to the workspace B.

PipelineStatus.description:
Indicate the status of the pipeline.
STOPPED: Pipeline is not running, not scheduled to work and has no state. Next start will start from scratch the pipeline.
RUNNING: Pipeline is running, scheduled to work and has a state.
INVALID: Should never happen, in case of the pipeline associated worker is removed or invalid.
TRANSITIONING: Pipeline is changing status (from stopped to running for example).
PAUSED: Pipeline is not running, not scheduled to work but has a state. Next start will resume from this state.
OPERATING: Pipeline is changing status (Status OPERATING is a value only used in widget and should not be set permanently).

PipelineState.description:
Indicate the state of the pipeline.
Status:
INACTIVE: When a pipeline is Inactive, the execution of the pipeline is either stop or suspended.
ACTIVE: When a pipeline is Activated, it is schedule to work.
INVALID: Should never happen, in case of the pipeline associated worker is removed or invalid.
TRANSITIONING: Pipeline is changing status (from inactive to active for example).
Flag:
WORKING: Pipeline is active, scheduled to work and is currently working : performing operations (ex : indexing order).
IDLE: Pipeline is active, scheduled to work and is currently not working, waiting for the next schedule.
PAUSED: Pipeline is inactive, not scheduled to work but has a state saved. Next start will resume from this state.
STOPPED: Pipeline is inactive, not scheduled to work and has no state. Next start will start from start the pipeline.
